title: pilosella auriculoides subsp. arvensis
intro: Pilosella auriculoides subsp. arvensis, also known as rough hawk's beard, is a flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family and is native to Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The rough hawk's beard has been reported to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting liver function. Herbal preparations of Pilosella auriculoides subsp. arvensis are commonly made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes.
